What's going on everyone? Summary metrics. So this is basically the bottom line of the bottom line and this is what I look at probably most closely on a regular basis. This is for me a daily thing and is honestly one of the most valuable practices that I have done in my life for making more money and increasing my net worth. So let's get into it. So total revenue, service net revenue plus retail net revenue. So that's, you could also call this total net revenue. But this is basically adding in all the money that was made from both lines or all the lines of revenue that you have, right? AKA what was billed during this time period successfully, right? That's after refunds and everything. It's like what's the real money that actually came in during this time period. Alright, total profit. So it's going to be a service profit plus your retail commissions earned, right? That's all you're doing. This is something that's harder to do on like a daily basis or a weekly basis. It's typically done better on a monthly basis. Retail commissions earned can be done on a daily basis because it's automatically calculated for you. But if you want to combine these on a monthly basis, it's probably going to be the easiest thing for you, which I'll cover in the next, when we get to the, the Excel sheets and the house stuff, or the where to where to do it stuff. Cash balance. This is one of the most valuable. Like if I had to have one stat that changed my life, this was it. Okay, so I should probably make this like super starred and whatnot. But this is the total of all your business accounts. Okay. I actually it's like being able to track this, I track this on a daily basis. And what happens is you, you see a big dip or you see a big pop and you're like, where did that come from? And you start to get a really amazing pulse on your business. Like, you know, you know, you start to know what days the balances go up, you start to know what days they go down, like why they go down and what reason it is. And the more closely tied you can be to this number and feel it, the more judicious and prudent you're going to be about the expenses you have and the things that you do to generate revenue. It's one of the single things that increase my network the most. And so I'd highly recommend tracking this on a daily basis. The next one is like net worth. And so I like these, the C and D are really like the big ones, right? Which is total the business accounts plus the total of your personal cash accounts, because a lot of times, most people on here, your business and you are very inextricable, you're, you're, you're tied together. And so, you know, if you take money out of the business, it goes to your personal like, but it's still kind of one big pool of cash. Unless you're a, you know, mid to large company, mid sizes, 100 plus employees, large would be 1000 plus employees. And so most people are watching us are not in that boat. And so you are tied to your business. And so having your liquid net worth. The reason I do it liquid is because like, I don't want the my stock index going up and down to have like, it's not going to affect your decisions, right? Your, your house valuations, not going to affect your decisions, right? But your liquid net worth, how much actual cash you have in your accounts is going to affect how you make decisions where you invest, how you like when you're going to make expenses, etc. All right. And that pretty much covers all of the stats that we are that I think you should focus on for your business.
